hero.description: I design and build AI-powered products from zero to one. Focused on making complex technology feel simple, useful, and beautiful—shipping fast and iterating in public.
hero.social_proof: Trusted by collaborators across AI, design & engineering

process.label: / How I work
process.headline: Path to shipped features
process.step1_desc: Mapping out the system constraints, data flow, and UI surface. Deciding exactly what to build and what not to build.
process.step2_desc: Deep focus engineering. Translating the mental model into solid Next.js and Python code with robust AI integrations.
process.step3_desc: Polishing the sharp edges. Adding micro-animations, optimizing performance, and listening closely to telemetry and user feedback.

about.metadata_desc: CEO & AI-Native Builder at Nebutra Intelligence. Building intelligent systems, not just products.
about.label: / About
about.headline: Building intelligent systems,
about.bio_1: I'm Tseka Luk, CEO of Nebutra Intelligence, based in Wuxi, China. I build AI-native products designed for global markets—tools that are not just technically sophisticated but genuinely useful and beautifully simple.
about.bio_2: My philosophy is what I call Vibe Business: build with taste, ship with speed, and let the product speak louder than any pitch deck. Every project starts from a real itch, and every feature earns its place.
about.bio_3: I think in systems, obsess over details, and believe the best way to predict the future is to build it—one commit at a time.
about.journey_2022: BSc in Information & Computing Science and BEng in Computer Science & Technology, Jiangsu Ocean University
about.journey_2025: CEO, Nebutra Intelligence — building for global markets
about.journey_now: Shipping AI-native products, one commit at a time
about.awards_description: Not an expert at anything — just someone who wants to try everything, and try it well.
about.award_1_detail: NSGA-III tourism optimizer, 8.3% prediction error vs. 28,000+ teams
about.award_4_detail: Pet industry forecast: LASSO R-squared=0.9850, predicted $52B market
about.award_5_detail: Biomass co-pyrolysis: found 28.44% optimal ratio via LightGBM + PSO
about.award_6_detail: 1,200+ samples, Claude + Flux + Midjourney AIGC pipeline
about.award_7_detail: Warehouse drone: 95% recognition accuracy, 3-min shelf traversal
about.award_8_detail: Real estate cost prediction: Random Forest outperformed CNN-LSTM
about.award_9_detail: Self-aiming system: adaptive PID + Kalman filter on MSPM0G3507
about.award_10_detail: Non-English major, highest national honor in bilingual translation
about.cred_5: 3 Software Copyrights (Power Opt, OD Classify, Graph Solver)
about.personality_intp_desc: Analytical, systematic, and driven by an insatiable curiosity about how systems really work.
about.personality_4w5_desc: Identity-driven depth and authentic self-expression, tempered by intellectual withdrawal.
about.belief_1: The people who are crazy enough to think they can change the world are the ones who do.

uses.label: / Uses
uses.headline: What I use to build.
uses.description: Hardware, software, and AI tools I actually use daily. Dogfooding included.
uses.ai_toolkit_desc: Tools I actively use — not a marketing list. I switch between them depending on the task.

privacy.metadata_desc: How this site handles your data.
privacy.headline: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This site is a personal portfolio. I respect your privacy and collect as little data as possible.
privacy.analytics_body: This site uses Plausible Analytics, a privacy-friendly, cookie-free analytics tool. No personal data is collected. All data is aggregated and anonymous. No data is shared with third parties.
privacy.auth_body: If you sign in (via GitHub, Google, or Linux DO), I receive your public profile information (name, avatar, email if available). This is used solely to identify you in the Soul chat. I do not sell, share, or use this data for any other purpose. Session data is stored as an encrypted JWT cookie in your browser.
privacy.chat_body: Messages you send in the Soul chat are processed by the Anthropic API (Claude) in real time and are not stored on my servers. Conversation history exists only in your browser session.
privacy.cookies_body: This site uses only essential cookies: a session cookie for authentication and a theme preference cookie. No tracking cookies are used.
privacy.third_party_body: Plausible Analytics (privacy-friendly, EU-hosted), Anthropic API (chat processing), Vercel (hosting). No advertising or tracking services are used.
privacy.contact_body: For any privacy-related questions, reach me at

guestbook.label: / Guestbook
guestbook.headline: Guestbook
guestbook.description: Leave a message, say hello, or share what you're building.
guestbook.sign_in_prompt: Sign in to leave a message.
guestbook.empty: No messages yet. Be the first!

any2md.tagline: Where other converters lose 40% of context, this one keeps 93%
any2md.description: Most converters silently drop formulas, tables, and cross-references. any2md uses a dual-engine—PyMuPDF for text + Qwen-VL for vision—then DeepSeek LLM reconstructs context via layered prompt + few-shot + CoT. Processes 68 pages/min with 85% table structuring and 90% LaTeX formula preservation. Imported 20,000+ pages into a team knowledge base in one week, cutting manual cleanup by 70%.

low-altitude-economy.tagline: Discovered a paradox: risk perception actually increases adoption
low-altitude-economy.description: Everyone assumed perceived risk would deter UAM adoption. Our TAM–SEM model on 2,609 respondents (93.1% recovery rate, KMO=0.951) proved the opposite: risk positively drives willingness to adopt (β=0.262, p<0.001). We also mined 1,695 Bilibili comments with SnowNLP to map public sentiment. The “risk paradox” finding challenged a decade of technology adoption literature. Provincial First Prize.

nebutra-sailor.tagline: One monorepo, 4 production apps, 12,000+ lines of shared code
nebutra-sailor.description: Enterprise-grade monorepo powering 4 apps (marketing, dashboard, docs, studio) from 6 shared packages. Next.js 16 + React 19 + Tailwind v4, with a custom design token pipeline, 541 tree-shakable icons, and multi-theme engine supporting 6 oklch color schemes. Ships features 3x faster than separate repos.

mcm-icm.tagline: Optimizing tourism vs. environment with 8.3% prediction error
mcm-icm.description: MCM/ICM 2025 (Problem B): built a system dynamics + NSGA-III multi-objective optimizer balancing tourist flow, environmental quality, and social satisfaction. Achieved 8.3% tourist prediction error with R²>0.5 on a three-dimensional coupled model. Competed against 28,000+ teams globally. Honorable Mention.

biomass-modeling.tagline: Found the exact 28.44% ratio—won Grand Innovation Award for it
biomass-modeling.description: Three-model ensemble (LightGBM + Gaussian Process Regression + PSO) tested across 500+ pyrolysis data points to discover that 28.44% biomass-to-coal ratio maximizes clean energy output with R²>0.95 prediction accuracy. The entropy-weighted fuzzy evaluation framework quantified non-linear interactions between 3 yield types (gas, char, liquid). Judges praised the methodology as transferable across energy domains. First Prize + Grand Innovation Award at Shuwei Cup.

pet-industry.tagline: LASSO R²=0.9850 predicted a $52B market by 2026
pet-industry.description: Built an integrated forecasting framework (ARIMAX-GARCH + VAR + LASSO + Prophet) spanning market size, pet population, and food manufacturing. LASSO regression identified key drivers with R²=0.9850. Cross-compared US/EU markets using HHI concentration index. Predicted 144.68M pets and 82.8% capacity utilization by 2026.

cursor-export.tagline: 86% less time on knowledge management—one-click conversation export
cursor-export.description: Cursor IDE extension (7 GitHub stars) that exports AI conversations to structured Markdown. Tested on 214 conversations (1,980 messages): processing time dropped from 5.8 min to 0.8 min per conversation, code snippet loss rate under 0.5%, throughput 12.5 conversations/min. Cross-platform VSIX with >98% install success rate across Windows/macOS/Linux.

brand-strategy.tagline: 1,200+ consumer samples × AIGC pipeline = 40% engagement lift
brand-strategy.description: Led a PEST-SWOT driven brand overhaul for Suzhou’s “林莓莓” agricultural product. Surveyed 1,200+ consumers to map purchase drivers and price sensitivity. Deployed Claude + Flux + Midjourney AIGC toolchain for brand stories and mascot concepts, cutting creative iteration from weeks to days. Projected 40%+ user engagement increase. National Second Prize.

mineru-skill.tagline: Zero-config Claude Code skill—PDF to Markdown in one command
mineru-skill.description: Open-source Claude Code skill that wraps MinerU for layout-aware PDF/document conversion. One-command install, no API keys, 3 supported formats (PDF, DOCX, PPTX). Handles 100+ page documents with table and formula preservation. Published on Smithery marketplace, 5+ community installs in first week. Built for the Claude Code ecosystem with auto-detection of document type and intelligent chunking.

warehouse-drone.tagline: 95% recognition accuracy, full shelf scan in under 3 minutes
warehouse-drone.description: Multi-rotor drone system for automated warehouse stocktaking. Integrated STM32 flight controller + OpenMV vision + BLE communication for autonomous waypoint navigation and QR code scanning across 3D shelf structures. 95% barcode recognition accuracy with laser calibration, completing full inventory traversal in under 3 minutes.

cdtmp.tagline: Task success rate +18pp by turning prompt tricks into reusable protocol
cdtmp.description: Turned ad-hoc prompt engineering into a structured agent protocol: task decomposition, state machines, retry strategies, and auditable intermediate states. Tested on 120 cross-domain tasks: success rate jumped from 63% to 81%, JSON compliance from 72% to 94%, rework rounds dropped 29%. Framework-agnostic—plugs into LangGraph, Semantic Kernel, or custom executors.

next-unicorn.tagline: Your codebase has 47 hand-rolled wheels—this finds them all
next-unicorn.description: Every codebase accumulates Vibe Coding debt: custom date formatters, DIY loggers, bespoke state machines. Next-Unicorn audits your code via Context7 MCP, identifies reinvented wheels, generates migration plans, and ships delete-code checklists. 176 tests passed with 29 property-based verifications. Published on Smithery + npm, supports 35+ AI agents including Claude Code, Cursor, and OpenCode.

hydrogem-web.tagline: Real-time water quality dashboard with 5-second refresh and alerts
hydrogem-web.description: React + TypeScript monitoring system tracking 12 water quality parameters (pH, turbidity, dissolved oxygen, etc.) with 5-second auto-refresh and configurable alert thresholds. Built with streaming data visualization and historical trend analysis for environmental monitoring.

ocr-auto.tagline: 100% bbox accuracy on 50 element types—from 65% to 92% in 10 prompt iterations
ocr-auto.description: Production-grade automated annotation system for document page elements. Async 4-stage pipeline with Qwen VL models identifies 50 element types (12 code languages, 13 interaction formats, 12 content elements, 13 other tags). Three-layer fault tolerance (Retry + RateLimit + CircuitBreaker), SHA256 content-addressed cache, and real-time SSE monitoring dashboard.

tiktok-pipeline.tagline: 8-dimensional annotation across 19 prompt iterations—edge cases included
tiktok-pipeline.description: Multi-modal visual search relevance labeling pipeline using LLMs. Processes image + text signals across 8 annotation dimensions (visual relevance, content relevance, query/doc drop, functional parity, category granularity, visual similarity, modal corroboration). 19 prompt versions with automated comparison and reflection analysis tools.

mlbb-analysis.tagline: 5 platforms, 6 batch processors, 90%+ accuracy—from manual review to automated pipeline
mlbb-analysis.description: Multi-platform video content analysis system covering YouTube, Instagram, TikTok, VK, and Facebook. Refactored from legacy monolith to modular architecture: 5 platform fetchers, 6 batch processors, 5 video analyzers. Fast text analysis achieves 90%+ accuracy with 460x acceleration over manual review, processing 1,207+ videos across batch runs.

synapse-quant.tagline: 8 microservices, real-time GraphQL, TUI cockpit—from raw data to trade thesis
synapse-quant.description: Open-source quantitative trading copilot for crypto markets. 8 microservices + 9 infrastructure services (17 containers total) with unified TUI cockpit, 41 technical indicators, real-time market data streaming, and in-context AI copilot. TypeScript 5.7 + Python 3.11+, CI/CD with GitHub Actions.

hm3d-eval.tagline: 4.62/5 evaluation score across 1,000 real 3D scenes—8/8 acceptance criteria passed
hm3d-eval.description: Comprehensive quality evaluation framework for the Habitat-Matterport 3D Research Dataset (1,000 real 3D scenes). Assessed 15+ dimensions including mesh quality, texture integrity, semantic annotation accuracy, and metadata completeness. Achieved 4.62/5 overall score with 100% acceptance pass rate (8/8 criteria). 95% requirement compliance.

appen-dashboard.tagline: Enterprise SaaS for a NASDAQ data company: the B2B demo mode playbook in production
appen-dashboard.description: Built multi-tenant reporting infrastructure for Appen (NASDAQ: APT). Next.js 16 monorepo (5 apps + 10 packages) serves TCS, ByteWorks, and DataPower dashboards with Upstash QStash async job queues and Pusher real-time. The breakthrough: a production-grade demo mode with MSW + tRPC mock interception, privacy redaction pipeline (schema validate → redact → normalize → value protocol), and 4 behavioral strategies—solving the #1 enterprise SaaS sales problem.

t2v-relabeling.tagline: Using better AI to fix worse AI's labeling mistakes—at the cost human reviewers can't match
t2v-relabeling.description: Meta-labeling pipeline that audits and corrects mislabeled AI-generated video training data using Gemini 2.5 Flash. Classifies 6 attributes (quality, gender, age, race, scene, season) per video with EWMA-adaptive rate limiting and HTTP/2 concurrency. Processes 60MB+ videos via Gemini Files API without local download. 3 iterative rounds with incremental merge. Human labeling: ~$1/video. This pipeline: ~$0.01/video at scale.

url-annotation.tagline: The TLS fingerprint arms race: when IP blocking fails, impersonate Chrome at the handshake
url-annotation.description: Production URL consistency pipeline that defeats TLS-fingerprint bot detection at zero marginal LLM cost. curl_cffi spoofs Chrome 124's full ClientHello (cipher suites, extensions, ordering)—not just User-Agent. Playwright renders JS-heavy SPAs. Local Qwen3:1.7b via Ollama compares semantics without API fees or GDPR exposure. Rotating-ad detection re-fetches 3× to flag domain changes. 30 HTTP + 5 Playwright + 5 LLM concurrent.

3dgs-eval.tagline: 3DGS had 2,000+ citations in 6 months but zero enterprise quality standards—we wrote the spec
3dgs-eval.description: First systematic vendor evaluation framework for 3D Gaussian Splatting content production. Multi-round scored assessment across 6 dimensions: HP/LP geometry (pivot + retopology), PBR texture completeness (BaseColor/Normal/Metallic/AO), file naming conventions, semantic annotation, preview standards (8+ renders), and communication SLA. The revelation: vendors weren't technically incapable—they were operationally immature. Multi-round CSV tracker makes every revision cycle auditable.
